care.summary: A dramatic calathea with large, round, dark green leaves featuring a feathered silver pattern and deep purple undersides.
care.light: Low to medium indirect light; direct sun will scorch the leaves
care.water: Keep soil evenly moist; use distilled or rainwater. Reduce slightly in winter
care.soil: Well-draining peat-based mix with perlite and orchid bark
care.humidity: High (60%+); essential for preventing crispy leaf edges
care.fertilizer: Dilute houseplant fertilizer every 4 weeks spring to early fall
common_issues: Brown crispy edges from low humidity or tap water
Yellow leaves from overwatering or cold drafts
Faded or washed-out pattern from excessive light
Leaf curling from underwatering or stress
tips: Never use tap water — mineral buildup causes leaf tip burn
Group with other humidity-loving plants to create a microclimate
Leaves fold up at night (prayer plant motion) — this is normal
Remove yellow leaves at the base to keep the plant tidy
